issues.body: The round-1 overclaim is corrected in the README and test documentation, but remains in the generator's provenance comments: the header defines each pair as `LEGAL (provable)` and calls dispatch.rs the source of truth for `which pairs are provable`. That is inconsistent with the same change's load-bearing caveat that dual-leaf host encoding is not implemented and resolve_circuit is not wired into verify_manifest. Since this script is the declared generator/source for the committed ZK artifact, its claims must use the same structural, unwired meaning as the README.
issues.fix_hint: Replace `LEGAL (provable)` with `LEGAL (dispatch-compatible under the unwired resolver rule)` and replace statements that dispatch identifies `which pairs are provable` with `which pairs the resolver structurally admits`. Add the explicit not-end-to-end-provable caveat to the generator header, matching the README and Rust test documentation. Also avoid `production_selectable` for dual-leaf unless it is narrowly defined as enum/registry selectable rather than operationally usable.
issues.title: Generator still equates design legality with provability

summary: Round 2 fixes the vacuous illegal-cell-cost check with deny_unknown_fields and a real mutation guard, and the README now clearly defines legal as dispatch-compatible rather than end-to-end provable. One honesty inconsistency remains in the generator itself: its load-bearing comments still equate LEGAL with provable and describe dispatch as the source of truth for which pairs are provable, even though the generated artifact and README acknowledge that dual-leaf host encoding is absent and resolver dispatch is unwired. Because this is a ZK comparison artifact and the generator is presented as its provenance, that contradictory claim should be corrected before approval.
